_comment: planner/architect mode (godplan) — additive + tightened. Adds planning sign-off claim words (plan ready/comprehensive/solid, ready to build, sound approach) and accepts CODEBASE-SURVEY actions as proof a plan is grounded: search/exploration commands (grep/rg/find/git-grep/tree) and the Grep/Glob/code-search tools. reReadClears:false so re-reading the plan you just wrote does NOT clear a 'ready' claim — you must have surveyed the REAL code. Pure in-response plans don't mutate files, so they're gate-exempt; the contract carries the grounding discipline.
_claimNote: Each entry is a VERDICT/sign-off on the plan you produced — not neutral description of existing or alternative code. Bare 'good|sound|clear design' (line-6 of the old set) over-matched descriptive prose ('the existing code has a clear design', 'a sound approach but adds a dependency'), so the adjective+noun form is now anchored to 'this is a …'. 'comprehensive|thorough plan' is a real completeness claim and is matched in natural order. The apostrophe class tolerates a typographic ' (U+2019).
_testCmdNote: Grounding = SEARCHING the real code (not catting your own plan). grep/rg/find/git-grep/tree and the Grep/Glob tools count; a bare re-read of the written plan does not (reReadClears:false).
